---
title: "Theorem 2.10 — Linear projection covariance formula"
lean_name: "HansenEconometrics.linearProjectionBeta_eq_covMat_inv_covVec"
chapter: 2
---

## Textbook statement

In the linear projection model

$$
Y = \alpha + X^\top\beta + e,
\qquad \operatorname{cov}(X,e)=0,
$$

the slope coefficient has the covariance formula

$$
\beta = \operatorname{Var}(X)^{-1}\operatorname{Cov}(X,Y).
$$

In Lean, the theorem assumes the
[Chapter 2 covariance and projection hypotheses](../../assumptions/chapter2.qmd):
finite second moments, a probability measure, invertibility of `covMat μ X`,
the pointwise affine model, and the orthogonality condition `covVec μ X e = 0`.

### Proof sketch

Take covariance with $X$ on both sides of the model:

$$
\operatorname{Cov}(X,Y)
= \operatorname{Cov}(X,\alpha + X^\top\beta + e)
= \operatorname{Var}(X)\beta + \operatorname{Cov}(X,e).
$$

The orthogonality condition sets $\operatorname{Cov}(X,e)=0$, so

$$
\operatorname{Cov}(X,Y)=\operatorname{Var}(X)\beta.
$$

The population normal-equations uniqueness theorem then identifies
$\beta$ with `linearProjectionBeta (covMat μ X) (covVec μ X Y)`.

## Lean statement

The theorem lives in
[`HansenEconometrics/Chapter2LinearProjection.lean`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter2LinearProjection.lean#L189):

::: {.lean-hover-panel}
```{=html}
<pre><code><span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="Covariance-form slope theorem for the linear projection model.">theorem linearProjectionBeta_eq_covMat_inv_covVec</span>
    [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
    <span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="Regressor vector X, response Y, error e, intercept α, and slope β.">(X : Ω → k → ℝ) (Y e : Ω → ℝ) (α : ℝ) (β : k → ℝ)</span>
    <span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="The covariance matrix of X is invertible.">[Invertible (covMat μ X)]</span>
    <span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="Each coordinate of X and the error have finite second moments.">(hX : ∀ i, MemLp (fun ω => X ω i) 2 μ)
    (he : MemLp e 2 μ)</span>
    <span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="Pointwise linear projection model.">(hmodel : Y = fun ω => α + dotProduct (X ω) β + e ω)</span>
    <span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="Projection-error orthogonality: cov(X,e)=0.">(horth : covVec μ X e = 0)</span> :
    <span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="The coefficient β equals covMat(X)⁻¹ covVec(X,Y), packaged as linearProjectionBeta.">β = linearProjectionBeta (covMat μ X) (covVec μ X Y)</span>
</code></pre>
```
:::

### Reading the statement

| Lean | Math | What it gives us |
|---|---|---|
| `[IsProbabilityMeasure μ]` | probability law | Needed for covariance and expectations. |
| `X : Ω → k → ℝ` | random vector $X$ | Regressor vector. |
| `Y e : Ω → ℝ` | $Y$, $e$ | Response and projection error. |
| `α : ℝ`, `β : k → ℝ` | intercept and slope | Parameters in the affine model. |
| `[Invertible (covMat μ X)]` | $\operatorname{Var}(X)$ invertible | Lets Lean form the covariance-based projection coefficient. |
| `hX`, `he` | finite second moments | Required for covariances. |
| `hmodel` | $Y=\alpha+X^\top\beta+e$ | Pointwise model identity. |
| `horth` | $\operatorname{Cov}(X,e)=0$ | Orthogonality condition that produces the normal equations. |
| conclusion | $\beta=\operatorname{Var}(X)^{-1}\operatorname{Cov}(X,Y)$ | Textbook covariance formula. |

## Translation notes

- `covMat μ X` is Hansen's $\operatorname{Var}(X)$.
- `covVec μ X Y` is Hansen's $\operatorname{Cov}(X,Y)$.
- `linearProjectionBeta (covMat μ X) (covVec μ X Y)` is the formal
  covariance formula $\operatorname{Var}(X)^{-1}\operatorname{Cov}(X,Y)$.
- The proof routes through
  [`linearProjectionBeta_eq_of_normal_equations`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter2LinearProjection.lean#L42):
  once `covMat μ X *ᵥ β = covVec μ X Y` is proved, uniqueness does the rest.

## Tactic-by-tactic walkthrough

::: {.lean-hover-panel}
```{=html}
<pre><code><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="The covariance-form slope theorem.">theorem linearProjectionBeta_eq_covMat_inv_covVec
    [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
    (X : Ω → k → ℝ) (Y e : Ω → ℝ) (α : ℝ) (β : k → ℝ)
    [Invertible (covMat μ X)]
    (hX : ∀ i, MemLp (fun ω => X ω i) 2 μ)
    (he : MemLp e 2 μ)
    (hmodel : Y = fun ω => α + dotProduct (X ω) β + e ω)
    (horth : covVec μ X e = 0) :
    β = linearProjectionBeta (covMat μ X) (covVec μ X Y) := by</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="Flip the goal to match the normal-equations uniqueness theorem, which returns linearProjectionBeta = candidate.">  symm</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="Use the theorem saying any b satisfying QXX b = QXY equals the projection coefficient.">  apply linearProjectionBeta_eq_of_normal_equations</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="Prove the normal-equation identity covVec(X,Y)=covMat(X)β+covVec(X,e).">  have hcov :
      covVec μ X Y = covMat μ X *ᵥ β + covVec μ X e := by</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="Rewrite Y by the affine model.">    rw [hmodel]</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="Apply the reusable affine-model covariance decomposition.">    exact covVec_linearProjectionModel (μ := μ) X e α β hX he</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="Finish the normal equations: the error covariance term is zero by orthogonality.">  calc
    covMat μ X *ᵥ β = covMat μ X *ᵥ β + covVec μ X e := by rw [horth]; simp
    _ = covVec μ X Y := by rw [hcov]</span>
</code></pre>
```
:::

## Downstream uses

- This theorem is the Chapter 2 population analogue of the closed-form OLS
  coefficient in Chapter 3.
- It also explains why covariance matrices are the natural inputs to later
  variance and robust-covariance formulas.
